title: Asuka Final death of author thread Split
display_title: Asuka Final death of author thread Split
wikitext:
[]: Asuka -> Final -> death of author thread [Split]             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 23, 2009 11:49 pm Definitely disagree. She's probably the most "normal" out of the main characters, but just because the others are flawed means that we're not supposed to like them?

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 2:00 am AuraTwilight wrote: Really, only Asuka puts up any real pretense of having self-love, and I've always felt that those who like Asuka have some form of mental illness, because I have never seen a human being more deserving of physically enforced misogyny in my entire life.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 24, 2009 2:46 am AuraTwilight wrote: I've always felt that those who like Asuka have some form of mental illness

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Oct 24, 2009 3:04 am AuraTwilight wrote: Therefore, we're not expected to sympathize and like them, but [...] fans just romanticize them.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Oct 24, 2009 3:06 am Alaska Slim wrote: She's arrogant about herself to an admitted fault, and yet unlike the many others whom do the same, she has reason to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 4:05 am Merridian wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: She's arrogant about herself to an admitted fault, and yet unlike the many others whom do the same, she has reason to be.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:42 am Mr. Tines wrote: Games People Play

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Oct 24, 2009 12:32 pm For all her fiery personality, she's shown to be tough as carbon-carbon evident in what it took to bring her down. She's arrogant about herself to an admitted fault, and yet unlike the many others whom do the same, she has reason to be. She's an exemplifying person of the trope she's supposed to fit, and yet at the same time a subversion. Her uniquness, is one of the first things I took notice of in Evangelion that set it apart in my mind from other shows of its kind. Didn’t like her at first, but she grew on me, once I figured out what made her tick. In short the reason I like her would be this, she’s acts more like a real person rather than the self-gratifying examples that came before and after her, and she doesn’t take the shit Shinji gives her, readily recognizing it for what it is. Anime itself could use a few more like her.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 2:10 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Oh, please, no. Every goddamned tsundere anime girl created after 1995 is a ripoff clone of Asuka to some extent.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 24, 2009 2:25 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Oh, please, no. Every goddamned tsundere anime girl created after 1995 is a ripoff clone of Asuka to some extent. Let the tsunedere fad die.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Oct 24, 2009 2:54 pm And all emotionless-girls are clones of Rei, and yet all are missing something that made them who they are. For Asuka, one thing would be just how much resistance she put up to her feelings for Shinji, and that she, justifiably so, hated him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 24, 2009 5:05 pm I don't really have much to say other than I feel Aura is making EXCELLENT points on both Asuka as well as Shinji in regards to their personalities and how they deal with other people. Ve really seems to "get" Eva compared to a few other people (Alaska and Tines, you guys are way off base). In regards to Rei, while she is meant to be creepy, I think you as the audience are meant to sympathize with her (not necessarily that she's moe), like with some of the rest of the cast. With the cast themselves, I guess it is a case of sympathizing with them, hating them, or even a mixture of both depending on the point of view.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 24, 2009 5:19 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Tines, you guys are way off base.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 5:27 pm AuraTwilight wrote: And all emotionless-girls are clones of Rei, and yet all are missing something that made them who they are. For Asuka, one thing would be just how much resistance she put up to her feelings for Shinji, and that she, justifiably so, hated him.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Oct 24, 2009 9:06 pm In regards to Rei, while she is meant to be creepy, I think you as the audience are meant to sympathize with her (not necessarily that she's moe), like with some of the rest of the cast.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:23 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Her Bitchitude existed since she first appeared in the series, and that's the Bitchitude I'm calling her out on.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:30 pm Methinks the thread subject needs to change to better reflect the current discussion, as it has nothing whatsoever to do with Shinji. Other than that, carry on.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Oct 25, 2009 12:51 am And you also called her on her "woe is me" attitude later on, and I responded. If you don't wish to speak on it, fine, don't bring it up.
